title: aconitum napellus f. carniolicum
intro: Aconitum napellus f. carniolicum, also known as the Alpine Monk's Hood or Alpine Monk's Head, is a subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, a perennial species in the buttercup family. This plant contains aconitine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain and inflammation. The medicinal properties of Aconitum napellus f. carniolicum have been used to promote health and well-being, including reducing fever and relieving rheumatic pain. Aconitum napellus f. carniolicum is traditionally prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use requires caution due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
